---
title: "Fetching large artifacts from OSF for reproducibility"
subtitle: "Design note: check-then-download cached models + simulation outputs"
date: 2026-06-23
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Problem

Anyone can clone/fork the repo, but the large derived artifacts are gitignored
and not in the clone:

| Folder | Size | Files | Read by the manuscript? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `output/sim_twostage/raw/` | 1.4 GB | 29,000 `.rds` | **No** — only to re-aggregate |
| `output/sim_twostage/draws_*.rds` | ~1.2 GB | 232 | **No** |
| `output/sim_twostage/per_sim_*.rds` | **11 MB** | 29 | **Yes** (`.load_screened()`) |
| `output/sim_twostage/{summary,diffs,diag}_*.rds` | small | — | partly |
| `output/data/fig4_summary.rds`, `fig5_hi_data.rds` | tiny | 2 | **Yes** |
| `output/models/*.rds` | **72 MB** | 38 | **Yes** (case-study fits) |

Dropbox is not a general solution (not everyone has it / has the quota). The
project already has a public OSF deposit:

- Node **`c6dxy`** — *"A flexible modelling framework for estimating thermal
  tolerance and sensitivity"* (public).
- Currently holds one file: **`sim_twostage.zip`, 2.68 GB**
  (md5 `4b019a10c52ad6c37e967cffd0cca2dc`), which bundles the whole
  `output/sim_twostage/` tree (raw + draws + aggregates).

## The key recommendation: tier the artifacts

Rendering the manuscript needs **~85 MB** (`per_sim_*` 11 MB + `output/data/`
+ `output/models/` 72 MB). The current single 2.68 GB zip forces every
figure-reproducer to download **30x** what they use. Split the OSF deposit into
self-contained zips by *purpose*:

| OSF zip | Contents | Size | Who needs it |
|---|---|---|---|
| `results.zip` | `output/sim_twostage/{per_sim,summary,diffs,diag}_*.rds` + `output/data/*.rds` | ~15 MB | **render the paper** (default) |
| `models.zip` | `output/models/*.rds` | 72 MB | re-render case studies without refitting |
| `sim_full.zip` | `raw/` (29k) + `draws_*.rds` | 2.6 GB | re-aggregate / re-derive from scratch |

Default `fetch` pulls `results.zip` + `models.zip` (~90 MB). `sim_full.zip` is
opt-in (`tier = "full"`). This is the single biggest usability win and it costs
only a re-zip + re-upload on the OSF side.

## Mechanism (no new package dependency)

`osfr` is not installed and not needed. The OSF v2 API exposes a direct download
link and an md5 per file, and `curl`/`httr`/`jsonlite` are already available:

- Resolve the file's download URL + md5 from
  `https://api.osf.io/v2/nodes/<node>/files/osfstorage/`.
- `curl::curl_download()` (resumable, handles multi-GB) to a temp file.
- Verify md5 against the manifest (reproducibility: you know you got the exact
  bytes), then `utils::unzip(exdir = <dest>)`.

**Check-then-download (idempotent).** Before downloading, test whether the
destination is already populated; skip if so (with a `force = TRUE` override).
Cheap check = "are the expected files present?"; strict check = "do their md5s
match the manifest?".

```{r fetch-sketch}
#| eval: false
# scripts/fetch_artifacts.R  (research-compendium infra; NOT package R/ code --
# we don't ship a 2.6 GB downloader inside bayesTLS)

# Manifest: one row per OSF zip. md5 pins the exact artifact set.
manifest <- tibble::tribble(
  ~zip,           ~node,    ~dest,                         ~tier,      ~md5,
  "results.zip",  "c6dxy",  here::here("output"),          "results",  "<md5>",
  "models.zip",   "c6dxy",  here::here("output", "models"),"results",  "<md5>",
  "sim_full.zip", "c6dxy",  here::here("output"),          "full",     "<md5>",
)

osf_file <- function(node, name) {
  j <- jsonlite::fromJSON(sprintf(
    "https://api.osf.io/v2/nodes/%s/files/osfstorage/", node))
  i <- which(j$data$attributes$name == name)
  list(url = j$data$links$download[i],
       md5 = j$data$attributes$extra$hashes$md5[i])
}

# A folder counts as "present" if it already holds the expected marker files.
have_it <- function(dest, marker_glob) length(Sys.glob(file.path(dest, marker_glob))) > 0

fetch_one <- function(row, force = FALSE) {
  if (!force && have_it(row$dest, row$marker)) {        # <- check-then-download
    message("skip (present): ", row$zip); return(invisible())
  }
  meta <- osf_file(row$node, row$zip)
  tmp  <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")
  curl::curl_download(meta$url, tmp, mode = "wb")        # resumable, multi-GB ok
  stopifnot(tools::md5sum(tmp) == row$md5)               # integrity gate
  utils::unzip(tmp, exdir = row$dest)                    # paths in zip are
  unlink(tmp)                                            #   relative to dest
}

fetch_artifacts <- function(tier = "results", force = FALSE) {
  rows <- manifest[manifest$tier %in% if (tier == "full") c("results","full")
                                      else tier, ]
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(rows))) fetch_one(rows[i, ], force = force)
}
```

**Zip internal layout matters.** Each zip must store paths *relative to its
`dest`* so `unzip(exdir = dest)` lands files in the right place. E.g.
`results.zip` should contain `sim_twostage/per_sim_scen3_*.rds`,
`data/fig4_summary.rds`, …; `models.zip` should contain bare `fit_*.rds`. Verify
with `unzip -l` after building each zip.

## Integration points

- **`make data`** (default tier) and **`make data-full`** targets calling
  `Rscript scripts/fetch_artifacts.R [results|full]`.
- **README** reproduction section: insert `make data` *before* `make ms` /
  before sourcing the figures.
- **Manuscript safety net (optional):** a setup chunk can call
  `fetch_artifacts("results")` so a fresh `quarto render` self-heals if the
  outputs are missing — but keep it `force = FALSE` so it never re-pulls on a
  populated tree.

## What needs whom

- **Buildable + testable now (me):** `scripts/fetch_artifacts.R`, the manifest
  scaffold, README + Makefile wiring. The download path is public, so the
  mechanism can be tested against the existing deposit (md5/url resolution; a
  real unzip without pulling all 2.68 GB by testing on the small zips once they
  exist).
- **Needs Daniel (OSF auth):** re-zip the deposit into `results.zip` /
  `models.zip` / `sim_full.zip`, upload, then I pin their md5s into the manifest.
  Uploading `output/models/` to OSF is new (the current deposit has only the sim
  tree).

## Decision (DN, 2026-06-23)

**Tiered deposit**, and **build the fetch script after the OSF upload** (so the
manifest can be pinned to real md5s and the full download path tested end-to-end
in one pass). Next action is on the OSF side (DN); script build is queued.

### Re-zip recipe (run from the repo root)

All three zips unzip into `output/`, so internal paths start at
`sim_twostage/…`, `data/…`, `models/…`. Building each zip from *inside*
`output/` produces exactly those relative paths:

```bash
cd output

# results.zip  (~15 MB) — everything the manuscript renders from
zip -r ../results.zip \
  sim_twostage/per_sim_*.rds \
  sim_twostage/summary_*.rds \
  sim_twostage/diffs_*.rds \
  sim_twostage/diag_*.rds \
  data/*.rds

# models.zip  (72 MB) — cached case-study brms fits (NEW to OSF)
zip -r ../models.zip models/*.rds

# sim_full.zip  (~2.6 GB) — raw per-sim files + posterior draws, for re-derivation
zip -r ../sim_full.zip \
  sim_twostage/raw \
  sim_twostage/draws_*.rds \
  sim_twostage/meta_*.rds
```

Verify before upload: `unzip -l results.zip | head` should show
`sim_twostage/per_sim_…` and `data/…` (NOT `output/sim_twostage/…`). Upload all
three to OSF node `c6dxy`; the existing `sim_twostage.zip` can be deleted (its
contents are now split across `results.zip` + `sim_full.zip`).

Then I: pin each zip's md5 into the manifest, write `scripts/fetch_artifacts.R`,
add `make data` / `make data-full`, wire the README, and test the full
check-then-download path end-to-end.

## Publishing (upload) workflow — built 2026-06-23

Rather than zipping + uploading by hand each time, publishing is automated and
idempotent so OSF always mirrors the latest local artifacts.

- **`scripts/osf_publish.R`** + **`make osf-push` / `make osf-status`**. For each
  tier it computes a cheap content signature (sorted `path + size + mtime` over
  the tier's globs), compares it to `data-raw/osf_manifest.json`, and re-zips +
  uploads **only the tiers that changed** since the last publish. The full tier's
  2.6 GB is therefore re-pushed only after an actual sim re-run, not on every
  build.
- **Token:** read from `OSF_PAT` (osf.full_write; in `~/.Renviron`). Never
  printed or written to disk. Missing token → a clear actionable error.
- **Upload mechanism:** OSF v2 API + waterbutler PUT (create new file, or PUT to
  an existing file's `links$upload` for a new version). No `osfr`/`osfclient`.
- **Manifest** (`data-raw/osf_manifest.json`, committed) records per tier:
  signature, OSF-reported zip md5, file count, size — and is the same file the
  fetch script will read for integrity checks.

**Verified without a token:** script parses; `make osf-status` reports
results 11.9 MB / models 72.6 MB / full 2.7 GB; the OSF read path resolves the
existing `sim_twostage.zip` (→ update branch) and a missing `results.zip`
(→ create branch); no-token run fails cleanly. **Untested until token present:**
the PUT itself.

**First-publish sequencing (once `OSF_PAT` is set):**
1. `make osf-push TIER="results models"` — the ~85 MB that lets the paper render;
   confirm both upload.
2. `make osf-push TIER=full` — the 2.6 GB re-derivation tier (one-time).
3. Delete the legacy `sim_twostage.zip` from OSF (now split into
   `results.zip` + `sim_full.zip`).

Two staged zips already built for a manual web-UI upload as a fallback:
`~/osf_c6dxy_upload/{results.zip (md5 e745d0ee…), models.zip (md5 c5ea62df…)}`.
